channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game Delayed Several Weeks
item.content:encoded:
Water, Earth, Fire, Air, those may be the 4 elements of bending, but the art of game bending knows a fifth: delay. An Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game delay is the latest title to adjust its release schedule to get everything right for its commercial debt, though thankfully this delay isn't too long.

What is the new Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game release date?

The new Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game release date is set for July 23rd, instead of the previous July 2nd, a three week delay. To help tide hungry Avatar fans over they will be able to try the game out on July 2nd as everyone who preorders on PC, PlayStation 5, and Xbox Series X|S will be able to try the game between July 2 and 5th in a cross-play enabled beta.

According to the developers announcement, the delay of Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game is to 'deliver the best possible experience', a rather vague statement but could imply there are some bugs that needed squashing. Additionally, they state they will use the time to add brand new content that was previously unplanned for the game.

Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game is only the latest game to be delayed, and this is a short delay as far as these things go. In recent days we've seen delays also strike Playground Games Fable, which was delayed essentially to avoid Grand Theft Auto VI and avoid competing with too many other Microsoft first party games, and Tomb Raider: Legacy of Atlantis which was delayed from this year to early next year, as announced during the PlayStation State of Play.

If this Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game lets them release the game in a more stable state, with possibly even a few extra cosmetics, I doubt that anyone will be complaining about it come August or September.

A longtime lover of speculative fiction, in almost all its forms, Don joined TechRaptor in 2014 on a whim sending in an application as he was looking for… More about Don



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/avatar-legends-fighting-game-delayed-several-weeks
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: This Summer's GM Fest 2026 Will Feature Nine Actual Plays by Nine Game Masters
item.content:encoded:
Fans of actual play productions have a new online event to look forward to. Comedian and professional Game Master Amy Vorpahl has announced GM Fest 2026, a new online event showcasing multiple actual plays showcasing the talent and showmanship of several new game masters.

The GM Fest 2026 Announcement

According to a press release, GM Fest 2026 will be a three-day livestream event. The event will be hosted by Amy Vorpahl, whose work in the tabletop industry includes writing credits for Dungeons & Dragons' Candlekeep Mysteries and Tasha's Cauldron of Everything. Vorpahl also runs an online "DM School," a 10-week program designed to improve Game Masters' improv skills, character creation, and refine their creative voice at a table.

The latter is important because GM Fest was born out of a desire by Vorpahl to give the spotlight to the students in that program. Complimenting Vorpahl's support is a partnership with Czepeku, the company behind the acclaimed Czepeku Maps and Tokens, and Alchemy RPG, the atmosphere-forward VTT platform.

 “The GMs who came through my class were all superstars, and I wanted to give them a place to showcase what they had learned. I hope the festival jump starts at least a few actual plays!”

This is an admirable gesture within the actual play ecosystem. There is still a major gap between multiple smaller creatives doing their own work and major household names like Brennan Lee Mulligan, Aabria Iyengar, and Jasmine Bhullar.

The GMs that will be showing off their talents include:

  • Safia Aldulaijan (@safiadulaijan)
  • Carrie Wogaman (@normalhumanadult)
  • JR Williams (@jrwilliamsiswriting)
  • Anastasia Hanchak (@anastasia.reverie)
  • Kimberly Barrante (@Kimberly.Barrante)
  • Dora Korpar (@d2k__)
  • Max deLaubenfels (@max.mildly.mad)
  • King Squishy (@mesartwork @thekingsquishy)
  • and of course Amy Vorpahl (@vorpahlsword)

There are also two substitute GMs to ensure every game gets played. They are Amanda Mata-Mei (@thathaglife) and Steven Nguyen (@sirnoogen)

GM Fest 2026 will start on June 12 -14. The event will contain nine livestreamed games starting at 10:30 AM PT, 1:00 PM PT, and 3:30 PM PT respectively. Eight of the livestreams will showcase eight different Game Masters running their choice of either Dungeons & Dragons 5e, 5.5e or Darrington Press' Daggerheart systems. 

The ninth and final event will be a game run by Amy Vorpahl using Daggerheart. All games will be run in the Alchemy VTT platform and feature maps and tokens by Czepeku.  All games will be livestreamed on both Vorpahl's Twitch and Alchemy's official YouTube channel.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/this-summers-gm-fest-2026-will-feature-nine-actual-plays-by-nine-game-masters
